_meta.purpose: Audit-driven reclassifications applied by build_frontier_map.py. Each entry corrects a vendored-CSV mathlib_area mis-tag: the corpus tagged many claims 'Mathlib.GroupTheory.OrderOfElement / Subgroup', which the classifier defaulted to status='tractable', even when the claim's actual content is a running-time complexity bound, a quantum (Shor/QFT) procedure, an index-calculus/Groebner step, a Weil pairing, an elliptic-net/EDS-period statement, or non-formalizable prose. These are status/foundation DEMOTIONS (and lateral moves) that the theorem-discharge overrides file intentionally does not carry. Applied after triage and before the verified/override paths, so a VERIFIED.md citation or a theorem-discharge override still wins.
_meta.provenance: Tractable-bucket over-optimism audit (2 independent strict skeptic agents over all 30 status='tractable' claims). 28 of 30 were mislabeled tractable purely from the lazy 'OrderOfElement / Subgroup' CSV area tag; each demotion re-derived from the claim's own statement content. 2 claims kept tractable (NOT listed here): glv-euclidean-basis-vector-015 (extended-Euclidean short kernel-lattice vector, pure number theory) and glv-frobenius-fast-example-004 (q-power Frobenius is a ring/curve endomorphism, elementary) — both genuinely formalizable now with no missing foundation.
_meta.note: foundation=null means the claim is non-formalizable prose (informal): paper-scope reportage, empirical 'Assumption N states ...', or algorithm-design description.
